---
title: "Reacting to an Exogenous Shock Mid-Forecast: A Demo Model with AEMO Electricity Demand Data"
description: "A demo forecasting model showing how to detect and respond to an exogenous shock partway through a forecast horizon, using AEMO electricity demand data."
date: 2026-06-24
categories: ["Forecasting (Econometric)", Python, "One-Off"]
image: "../images/ols_vs_hybrid_threshold_comparison.png"
---

## Overview

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor
incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

![OLS vs Hybrid-GAM: how quickly would each have flagged the COVID break?](../images/ols_vs_hybrid_threshold_comparison.png)

## Method

Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut
aliquip ex ea commodo consequat:

1. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
2. Consectetur adipiscing elit
3. Duis aute irure dolor in reprehenderit

## Demo Video

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/REPLACE_WITH_VIDEO_ID >}}

## Results

Excepteur sint occaecat cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia
deserunt mollit anim id est laborum.

## Links

- [Full analysis notebook](aemo-exogenous-shock-notebook.html)
- [GitHub repo](https://github.com/GG-makes)
- [AEMO data source](https://www.aemo.com.au/)
